title: Labelling observations in animate_xy
text: Pass character vector length number rows data. element label drawn next corresponding point. Turning axes keeps plot uncluttered labels present. Row numbers make reasonable default label let trace point back specific row data frame.
code: animate_xy(flea[,1:6],   obs_labels = as.character(1:nrow(flea)),   axes = "off" )

title: Labelling observations in animate_xy
text: Labelling every point can become hard read larger datasets. label subset, supply empty string "" observations want leave unlabelled. non-empty strings drawn. label just five extreme outliers, identified advance Mahalanobis distance:
code: # Find the five points furthest from the multivariate centre d2    <- mahalanobis(flea[,1:6], colMeans(flea[,1:6]), cov(flea[,1:6])) top5  <- order(d2, decreasing = TRUE)[1:5]  # Build a label vector: empty for most points, row number for the top 5 lbls <- rep("", nrow(flea)) lbls[top5] <- as.character(top5)  animate_xy(flea[,1:6], obs_labels = lbls, axes="bottomleft")

title: Labelling observations in animate_xy
text: Labels col argument work independently, can colour group still labelling individual points. common workflow colour observations grouping variable label points want call name.
code: # Colour by species; label only row 1 and row 22 (arbitrary examples) lbls <- rep("", nrow(flea)) lbls[c(1, 22)] <- rownames(flea)[c(1, 22)]  animate_xy(flea[,1:6],   col       = flea$species,   obs_labels = lbls,   axes      = "bottomleft" )

title: Tour types
text: grand tour generates target projections sampling uniformly space d-dimensional planes interpolates smoothly . time visits every possible projection, giving global overview data. right starting point know structure look . Points stand apart main cloud many projections likely outliers. Groups points stay together many projections, occasionally merge others, suggest clusters. grand tour samples randomly eventually show every view, may take stumble revealing one — guided tour helps.
code: # 2D projections — the default f <- flea[,1:6] # For convenience animate_xy(f)  # 1D projections shown as a density animate_dist(f)

title: Tour types
text: guided tour adds direction search: uses projection pursuit move towards projections score highly chosen index function. new target random chosen increase index, tour makes progress towards structure care rather wandering random. index function key choice: holes() looks projections low density near centre high density near edges — signature separated clusters. cmass() looks projections high density near centre — useful finding outliers sit away dense core. lda_pp() pda_pp() use known class labels find projections groups well separated. guided tour terminates better projection can found, stop . final view settles usually revealing one index. stops quickly — without finding visible structure — try sphere = TRUE animate() remove linear associations first, start different random seed.
code: # Find cluster structure without class labels animate_xy(f, tour_path = guided_tour(holes()))  # Same search, colour by known species once structure is found animate_xy(f,   tour_path = guided_tour(holes()),   col = flea$species )  # Use class labels directly to separate groups animate_xy(f,   tour_path = guided_tour(lda_pp(flea$species)),   col = flea$species )

title: Tour types
text: little tour planned tour axis-parallel projections: cycles every pair original variables turn. makes systematic way survey marginal 2D views data, equivalent watching slow animated scatterplot matrix. little tour visits axis-parallel projections can miss structure lives oblique directions — cluster separates along diagonal appear. best used sanity check complement grand tour rather primary exploration tool.
code: animate_xy(f, tour_path = little_tour())

title: Tour types
text: planned tour replays sequence bases already saved. typical workflow run grand tour save_history(), keep saved path, replay — perhaps different display, colour added fact. planned tour also share specific tour someone else: save history save_history(), save object disk saveRDS(), send alongside code.
code: # Save a grand tour path set.seed(42) t1 <- save_history(f, max = 10)  # Replay it in a scatterplot animate_xy(f, tour_path = planned_tour(t1))  # Replay the same path with species colour added animate_xy(f,   tour_path = planned_tour(t1),   col = flea$species )  # Cycle continuously through the saved bases animate_xy(f, tour_path = planned_tour(t1, cycle = TRUE))

title: Tour types
text: local tour makes small movements around chosen starting projection. step picks new target within specified angular distance (angle, radians) current position, view never strays far started. useful examining whether pattern spotted robust — structure survives small perturbations projection real feature rather coincidence particular viewing angle. tighter angle keeps view closer starting point. wider angle effectively becomes grand tour. Values around pi/4 pi/8 reasonable starting range.
code: # Start from a specific projection (e.g. one saved from a guided tour) start <- basis_random(6, 2)  # Explore a small neighbourhood of angle pi/4 animate_xy(f,   tour_path = local_tour(start, angle = pi / 4),   col = flea$species )

title: Tour types
text: frozen tour holds projection coefficients variables fixed specified values lets remaining coefficients vary freely grand tour. useful already know particular variable direction important want explore remaining variation keeping variable anchored view. Frozen values specified matrix NA numeric entries: NA means coefficient varies freely; number fixes . frozen tour specialised tour types useful domain knowledge particular variable always visible — example, verifying hypothesis role specific measurement.
code: # Fix variable 3 to contribute equally to both axes frozen      <- matrix(NA, nrow = 6, ncol = 2) frozen[3, ] <- 0.5  animate_xy(f, tour_path = frozen_tour(2, frozen))

code: # You can use a saved history to replay tours with different visualisations  t1 <- save_history(flea[, 1:6], max = 3) #> Converting input data to the required matrix format. animate_xy(flea[, 1:6], planned_tour(t1)) #> Converting input data to the required matrix format. #> Using half_range 4.4   ## andrews_history(t1) ## andrews_history(interpolate(t1))  ## t1 <- save_history(flea[, 1:6], grand_tour(4), max = 3) ## animate_pcp(flea[, 1:6], planned_tour(t1)) ## animate_scatmat(flea[, 1:6], planned_tour(t1))  ## t1 <- save_history(flea[, 1:6], grand_tour(1), max = 3) ## animate_dist(flea[, 1:6], planned_tour(t1))  testdata <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 3), ncol = 3) testdata[1:50, 1] <- testdata[1:50, 1] + 10 testdata <- sphere_data(testdata) t2 <- save_history(testdata, guided_tour(holes()),   max = 5 ) #> Target: 0.896, 11.5% better  #> Target: 0.908, 1.3% better  #> No better bases found after 25 tries.  Giving up. #> Final projection:  #> -0.268   0.963   #> -0.865  -0.236   #>  0.423   0.128   animate_xy(testdata, planned_tour(t2)) #> Using half_range 3.4    # Or you can use saved histories to visualise the path that the tour took. plot(path_index(interpolate(t2), holes()))   # And you can plot any individual frame using best_prj <- matrix(t2[,,3], ncol=2) p <- render_proj(testdata, best_prj) # which creates a data frame with the elements # to make the plot, see render_proj() for plotting code # OR see draw_tour_axes() for similar code in base graphics

what: Search for a better projection using simulated annealing — search_better_random
title: Search for a better projection using simulated annealing — search_better_random
text: Given initial \(t0\), cooling scheme updates temperature $$T = t0 /\log(+ 1)$$ candidate basis sampled via $$B_j = (1 - \alpha) * B_i + \alpha * B$$ alpha defines neighbourhood, \(B_i\) current basis, B randomly generated basis acceptance probability calculated $$prob = \exp{-abs((B_i) - (B_j))/ T}$$ information, see https://projecteuclid.org/download/pdf_1/euclid.ss/1177011077

title: tourr 0.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-12-11 Two vignettes added help users get started. Little tour now cycles twice variable list, randomised order. Two new projection pursuit indexes added, based function archived package mbgraphic. slice tour now available using function animate_slice(). new index searching interesting slices available. principal component analysis tour display now available, renders axes original variables. requires appropriate scaling data ahead time. See example code. new record option available print diagnostic information every step. Bind animate code name store record object. ferrn package https://github.com/huizezhang-sherry/ferrn can used diagnose optimisation techniques. Improvements optimisation functions made: search_better interrupt higher index achieved mid-path; search_polish takes best basis another routine, makes small steps get best projection close . Set default fps 10, compatibly draw RStudio window.
